gaps.description: The Mission Statement Protocol contains no substantive rules. Nomos (#200) has nothing to evaluate at Action Proposal adjudication, so all actions are authorized by default. This defeats the purpose of the MSP as the operational constitution.

The MSP amendment protocol requires that rules can only be created or modified when a Policy Exception Message (PEM) is generated by Nomos after blocking an Action Proposal. Since no rules exist, no AP can be blocked, so no PEM will ever be issued. This makes the `PEM → review → Rollout` pipeline inapplicable for adding the very first operational rules.

The Bill of Rights is in scaffold state with Articles I–VI undefined. Arbiter (#190) is required to verify the BoR at every scope query, but with no content it provides no actual perimeter. This means any action is within 'scope,' even though the MSP is subordinate to a non-existent boundary.

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "gap-msp-001",
      "title": "MSP lacks any mission objectives, invariants, or rules",
      "description": "The Mission Statement Protocol (1.0.0-seed) contains no substantive objectives, priority programs, OKRs, constraints, or safety rules. Nomos (#200) has no operational guidance for evaluating action proposals. Without mission objectives, there is no strategic framework to govern memory/context formation, force dream-cycle prioritization, or ensure the DIO’s memory systems align with a defined purpose. The dream-cycle health is therefore unmoored from strategic intent.",
      "msp_section": "MSP – Rules (empty) and Purpose (declares MSP to be the operational constitution, but none provided)",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "very_high",
      "strategic_importance": "The MSP is supposed to be the machine-actionable set of objectives that Nomos enforces. Its absence means there is no basis to determine what memories and contexts are strategically valuable, leading to aimless accumulation or neglect.     },

      "id": "gap-bor-001",
      "title": "Bill of Rights is entirely placeholder with no human-authored content",

      "urgency": "high",

      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "strategic_importance": "Memory consolidation (dreamer Phase 2) and context triage (dreamer Phase 1) rely on organ-specific rules to know what to promote, merge, or prune.       "msp_section": "MSP – Purpose (implies machine-actionable objectives that Nomos evaluates; dream-cycle output would be subject to such evaluation, but without it, the cycle’s absence goes unnoticed)",

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "OP-GAP-001",
      "description": "Complete absence of operational SLA definitions in the MSP and BoR. The MSP 'Rules' list is empty; the Bill of Rights (Articles I–VI) is entirely scaffold. No availability targets, latency SLOs, error budgets, or cadence baselines exist.",
      "grounding": "MSP Section 'Rules' (empty), BoR Article I 'Scope of Enterprise' (undefined), MSP 'Purpose' statement that it must translate BoR into machine-actionable objectives.",
      "priority": "P1",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "The DIO operates without any service quality floor. There are no thresholds to detect degradation, trigger PEMs, or guide Senate amendments. All other operational gaps derive from this absence."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "OP-GAP-002",
      "description": "No health-check cadence or recovery targets are defined. The spine-state data shows a fixed ~30-second interval where all organs simultaneously transition to DEGRADED and then recover within the same second. There is no rule governing acceptable ping interval, missed-pong timeout, or maximum DEGRADED duration, nor any constraint on synchronous transitions.",
      "grounding": "MSP Section 'Genome References' (organ-specific operating rules under genome/ are missing); BoR Article I (absent) would normally define operational availability standards; MSP 'Rules' section is empty.",
      "priority": "P2",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "The observed all-organ flapping occurs regularly, potentially causing intermittent unavailability of compliance checks (Nomos gates), memory operations (Hippocampus), and external integrations. Without a defined baseline, this behavior cannot be classified as anomaly or normal, and no automated correction or escalation triggers."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "OP-GAP-003",
      "description": "No organ availability SLA (e.g., maximum DEGRADED state duration, maximum allowed transitions per hour). The spine slice shows tens of DEGRADED->ALIVE transitions for every organ within minutes. Without a target, there is no quantitative expectation for organ uptime or stability.",
      "grounding": "MSP Section 'Rules' (empty); BoR Article II 'Prohibited Activities' is undefined, which could otherwise specify forbidden operational patterns like excessive flip-flopping.",
      "priority": "P3",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "Performance of components like Nomos (#200) and Arbiter (#190) relies on organ availability. Unbounded flapping could delay action proposal adjudication and erode system reliability, but without an SLA metric the severity cannot be measured or managed."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "OP-GAP-004",
      "description": "No runtime targets or SLOs for Action Proposal evaluation latency by Nomos (#200). The MSP is silent on how quickly the two compliance gates (Arbiter BoR scope check and MSP+genome rule evaluation) must complete.",
      "grounding": "MSP Section 'Purpose' describes Nomos’s two-gate process but does not specify timing constraints; BoR Article IV 'Human Authority Reservation' is undefined, which could set expectation on responsiveness; 'Genome References' section is empty.",
      "priority": "P4",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "low",
      "impact": "If gate latency grows excessively, action proposals may stall, impacting the DIO’s ability to react to events. Currently, no metric exists to detect or throttle long-tail evaluations."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "OP-GAP-005",
      "description": "No rule for critical incident classification when all organs become DEGRADED simultaneously. The observed pattern (every 30 seconds, 25+ organs flip together) indicates a systemic health-check single point of dependency or a shared resource issue. Without a trigger in the MSP, no PEM is generated to alert the Senate or human principal.",
      "grounding": "MSP Section 'Amendment Protocol' describes PEM-driven rule changes but does not define what constitutes a PEM-generating condition; BoR Article III 'Core Identity' (undefined) could define incident response posture; MSP 'Relationship to the Bill of Rights' requires BoR conflict detection, but no conflict arises from all-organ flapping without a rule.",
      "priority": "P5",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "A simultaneous global DEGRADED event could temporarily disable all compliance and coordination functions, yet the system would not escalate or log an exception. This gap leaves a critical operational blind spot."
    }
  ]
}
